title: The political left is disappearing across Central Europe, despite Orban's defeat in Hungary
subhed: Social democrats no longer have any MPs in Hungary, Bulgaria or the Czech Republic; have been pushed into opposition in Slovenia; and are polling at less than 10% in Poland. Often originating from former communist parties, they first lost working-class voters to more nationalist groups, and then, in the 2010s, lost the middle class.

title: Ban on toxic chemicals stalled by EU's 'limited progress'
subhed: The European Environmental Bureau and ClientEarth have considered the number of cases piling up on the European Commission's desk to be 'illegal and unacceptable' in a new report.

title: Les Flammes 2026: Theodora triumphs as Gims and Werenoi also honored
subhed: More polished and better organized, Thursday's ceremony for French popular music crowned Theodora as Best Female Artist. Gims took home the award for Best Male Artist, while Hamza won Best Song of the Year.

title: 'How can anyone love Céline Dion? The hysteria surrounding her Paris concerts is astonishing'
subhed: After the spectacular sale of nearly 500,000 tickets for a series of shows in Paris this autumn, Le Monde columnist Michel Guerrin questions in his column what drives people to love (or hate) the singer.

subhed: The death of a 14-year-old boy shot dead on Tuesday outside his school in Al-Mughayyir marks a new peak in an unprecedented spiral of violence in the West Bank. title: Ex-PM Attal distances himself from Macron in new memoir
subhed: In his first book, 'As a Free Man', Gabriel Attal retraces his personal journey and reflects on the French president's coldness toward him when he was prime minister.

title: Léon Marchand prepares return to French pools: 'I always told myself the Paris Games were just one step'
subhed: The 23-year-old swimmer, who won four Olympic golds in 2024, is set to compete at the French Championships in Saint-Etienne in July, followed by the European Championships in August in Saint-Denis.
